# Fluxis TypeScript SDK — Integration Guide

> This document is optimized for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, etc.).
> It contains everything needed to integrate Fluxis crypto payments into a TypeScript/Node.js project.

## What is Fluxis?

Fluxis is a crypto payment processing platform. Merchants use Fluxis to accept cryptocurrency payments.
The SDK wraps the Fluxis REST API and handles authentication, token refresh, and type conversion automatically.

- Package: `@fluxisus/sdk`
- Runtime: Node.js 18+ (uses native `fetch`, `AbortSignal.timeout()`, and `crypto.subtle`)
- Types: Full TypeScript support, strict mode
- Zero dependencies

## Installation

```bash
npm install @fluxisus/sdk
```

## Initialization

> **IMPORTANT — Server-only:** `FluxisClient` holds your `apiKey` and `apiSecret`. It must ONLY be instantiated in server-side code (Node.js, Next.js Server Components, Route Handlers, Server Actions). **Never import or instantiate `FluxisClient` in client-side / browser code** — this would expose your credentials in the JavaScript bundle sent to the browser.
>
> In Next.js projects, add `import 'server-only'` at the top of the file where you create the client. This makes Next.js throw a **build-time error** if the file is ever accidentally imported from a Client Component (`'use client'`).

```typescript
import 'server-only';           // Next.js: prevents accidental client-side import
import { FluxisClient } from '@fluxisus/sdk';

const fluxis = new FluxisClient({
  apiKey: 'fxs.stg.xxx',       // API key from Fluxis dashboard
  apiSecret: 'your-api-secret', // API secret (never expose publicly)
  environment: 'staging',       // 'staging' or 'production'
});
```

### Environments

| Environment | Base URL | API Key Prefix |
|-------------|----------|----------------|
| `staging` | `https://api.stgfluxis.us/v1` | `fxs.stg.*` |
| `production` | `https://api.fluxis.us/v1` | `fxs.prd.*` |

Default is `staging`. The SDK authenticates lazily on the first request and refreshes the token automatically ~60 seconds before it expires. If multiple requests fire concurrently while the token is being refreshed, only one auth call is made (deduplication). If a request receives a 401 (e.g. token revoked server-side), the SDK invalidates the cached token, re-authenticates, and retries the request once.

You can also pass a custom `baseUrl` to override the environment-based URL:

```typescript
const fluxis = new FluxisClient({
  apiKey: 'fxs.stg.xxx',
  apiSecret: 'secret',
  baseUrl: 'https://custom-api.example.com/v1',
});
```

---

## Flow 1: Direct Crypto Payment (NASPIP Token)

Use this when you know which crypto asset the customer will pay with.
Returns a NASPIP token that can be rendered as a QR code.

```typescript
import { FluxisClient } from '@fluxisus/sdk';

const fluxis = new FluxisClient({
  apiKey: 'fxs.stg.xxx',
  apiSecret: 'your-secret',
  environment: 'staging',
});

// Step 1: Create a Point of Sale (do this once, reuse the ID)
const pos = await fluxis.pointOfSale.create({
  name: 'Online Store',
  merchant: { name: 'My Shop', description: 'E-commerce store' },
  paymentOptions: ['npolygon_t0x3c499c542cEF5E3811e1192ce70d8cC03d5c3359'], // USDC on Polygon
});

// Step 2: Create a payment request
const payment = await fluxis.pointOfSale.createPaymentRequest(pos.id, {
  amount: '25.00',
  uniqueAssetId: 'npolygon_t0x3c499c542cEF5E3811e1192ce70d8cC03d5c3359',
  referenceId: 'order-001', // your internal order ID
});

// Step 3: Use the NASPIP token
console.log(payment.token);      // NASPIP token string — render as QR code
console.log(payment.id);         // Fluxis payment request ID
console.log(payment.status);     // "created"
console.log(payment.expiration); // Unix timestamp when it expires
```

### Unique Asset IDs

Format: `n{network}_t{tokenAddress}`

Common assets:
- USDC on Polygon: `npolygon_t0x3c499c542cEF5E3811e1192ce70d8cC03d5c3359`
- USDT on Polygon: `npolygon_t0xc2132D05D31c914a87C6611C10748AEb04B58e8F`

---

## Flow 2: Hosted Checkout (Reference Currency)

Use this for e-commerce where you specify an amount in a reference currency (e.g., USD). The customer sees "pay 49.99 USD" but the actual payment is processed in crypto (USDT, USDC, etc.) based on the `paymentOptions` configured on the PoS. Fluxis does NOT process fiat — `coinCode` is only used for display purposes.

```typescript
const checkout = await fluxis.pointOfSale.createPaymentRequestCheckout(pos.id, {
  amount: '49.99',
  coinCode: 'USD', // reference currency for display (payment is always in crypto)
  referenceId: 'order-002',
  order: {
    total: '49.99',
    coinCode: 'USD',
    description: 'Premium subscription',
    merchant: { name: 'My Store' },
    items: [
      {
        description: 'Premium Plan - Monthly',
        quantity: 1,
        unitPrice: '49.99',
        amount: '49.99',
        coinCode: 'USD',
      },
    ],
  },
});

console.log(checkout.id);          // Payment request ID
console.log(checkout.status);      // "created"
console.log(checkout.token);       // NASPIP token
console.log(checkout.checkoutUrl); // Hosted checkout page URL
```

---

## Flow 3: Webhooks

Fluxis sends payment status updates via webhooks. This is the primary mechanism for knowing when a payment is completed.

### Step 1: Configure webhook on your PoS

```typescript
const webhook = await fluxis.pointOfSale.createNotifications(pos.id, {
  webhookUrl: 'https://yourserver.com/webhooks/fluxis',
});

// IMPORTANT: Save this secret securely — it's only returned once
const webhookSecret = webhook.secret;
```

### Step 2: Receive and verify webhooks in your server

```typescript
import express from 'express';
import { verifyWebhookSignature } from '@fluxisus/sdk';

const app = express();

// IMPORTANT: Use raw body for signature verification
app.post('/webhooks/fluxis', express.raw({ type: 'application/json' }), async (req, res) => {
  const signature = req.headers['x-fluxis-signature'] as string;
  const rawBody = req.body.toString();

  const isValid = await verifyWebhookSignature(rawBody, signature, webhookSecret);

  if (!isValid) {
    return res.status(401).send('Invalid signature');
  }

  const event = JSON.parse(rawBody);

  // Process the payment status update
  switch (event.status) {
    case 'completed':
      // Payment received in full — fulfill the order
      console.log('Payment completed:', event.id);
      break;
    case 'overpaid':
      // Customer sent more than requested — fulfill + consider refund
      console.log('Payment overpaid:', event.id);
      break;
    case 'underpaid':
      // Customer sent less than requested
      console.log('Payment underpaid:', event.id);
      break;
    case 'expired':
      // Payment window expired with no payment
      console.log('Payment expired:', event.id);
      break;
    case 'failed':
      // Payment processing failed
      console.log('Payment failed:', event.id);
      break;
  }

  res.status(200).send('OK');
});

app.listen(3000);
```

### Webhook in Next.js App Router

```typescript
import { verifyWebhookSignature } from '@fluxisus/sdk';

export async function POST(request: Request) {
  const rawBody = await request.text();
  const signature = request.headers.get('x-fluxis-signature') || '';

  const isValid = await verifyWebhookSignature(rawBody, signature, WEBHOOK_SECRET);
  if (!isValid) return new Response('Invalid signature', { status: 401 });

  const event = JSON.parse(rawBody);
  // Handle event...
  return new Response('OK', { status: 200 });
}
```

**Important:** Signature verification requires the raw request body as a string. If your framework auto-parses JSON, preserve the raw body (use `express.raw()` in Express, `request.text()` in Next.js).

### Step 3: Update webhook URL (if needed)

```typescript
await fluxis.pointOfSale.updateNotifications(pos.id, {
  webhookUrl: 'https://newserver.com/webhooks/fluxis',
});
```

---

## Flow 4: Refunds

Refunds can be issued for completed or overpaid payments.

```typescript
// Full refund
const refund = await fluxis.refunds.create(paymentRequestId, {
  refundToAddress: '0x1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef12345678',
  reason: 'Customer requested',
});

// Partial refund
const partialRefund = await fluxis.refunds.create(paymentRequestId, {
  refundToAddress: '0x1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef12345678',
  amount: '10.00',
  reason: 'Partial refund for returned item',
});

// Check refund status
const detail = await fluxis.refunds.get(refund.id);
console.log(detail.status); // "processing" | "completed" | "failed"
```

---

## SDK Method Reference

### fluxis.accounts

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `list()` | List all accounts |
| `get(accountId)` | Get account by ID |
| `create({ name, externalId? })` | Create account |
| `update(accountId, { name?, externalId? })` | Update account |
| `delete(accountId)` | Delete account |
| `getSettlementAddresses(accountId)` | Get settlement addresses |

### fluxis.organization

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `setSettlementAddresses(addresses[])` | Set settlement addresses |
| `updateSettlementAddresses(addresses[])` | Update settlement addresses |

Each address: `{ address: string, network: string, addressTag?: string }`

### fluxis.pointOfSale

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `list()` | List all PoS |
| `get(posId)` | Get PoS by ID |
| `create({ name, accountId?, merchant?, paymentOptions? })` | Create PoS |
| `update(posId, { name?, merchant?, paymentOptions? })` | Update PoS |
| `getNotifications(posId)` | Get webhook settings |
| `createNotifications(posId, { webhookUrl })` | Create webhook (returns secret) |
| `updateNotifications(posId, { webhookUrl })` | Update webhook URL |
| `createPaymentRequest(posId, { amount, uniqueAssetId, referenceId?, order? })` | Create payment request → `PaymentRequestResponse` |
| `getPaymentRequest(posId, paymentRequestId)` | Get payment request status → `PaymentRequestResponse` |
| `createPaymentRequestCheckout(posId, { amount, coinCode, referenceId?, order? })` | Create checkout → `PaymentRequestCheckoutResponse` (includes `checkoutUrl`) |

### fluxis.naspip

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `create({ payment })` | Create NASPIP token from raw payment data |
| `read(token)` | Decode and verify a NASPIP token |
| `isValidTokenFormat(token)` | Check if string starts with `v4.local.` |

### fluxis.refunds

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `create(paymentRequestId, { refundToAddress, amount?, reason? })` | Create refund |
| `get(refundId)` | Get refund details |

### fluxis.transactions

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `list({ limit?, offset?, status?, sort?, order?, accountId? })` | List transactions |

---

## Types and Enums

### Payment Request Status

| Status | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `created` | Just created, awaiting payment |
| `processing` | Deposit detected, confirming on-chain |
| `completed` | Fully paid |
| `overpaid` | Received more than requested |
| `underpaid` | Received less than requested |
| `expired` | NASPIP token expired, no payment received |
| `failed` | Payment processing failed |

### Transaction Types

`deposit`, `withdraw`, `refund`, `adjustment`, `swap`, `payment_in`, `payment_out`, `dry_run`

### Transaction Statuses

`preview`, `pending`, `created`, `processing`, `error`, `expired`, `failed`, `completed`

---

## Error Handling

The SDK throws typed errors. Error messages automatically include the HTTP method and path for easier debugging (e.g. `"POST /pos/xxx/payment-request: Invalid amount"`).

```typescript
import {
  FluxisError,
  FluxisAuthError,
  FluxisNetworkError,
  FluxisResponseParseError,
} from '@fluxisus/sdk';

try {
  const payment = await fluxis.pointOfSale.createPaymentRequest(posId, {
    amount: '25.00',
    uniqueAssetId: 'npolygon_t0x3c499c542cEF5E3811e1192ce70d8cC03d5c3359',
  });
} catch (error) {
  if (error instanceof FluxisAuthError) {
    // API key/secret invalid, or token refresh failed
    // error.code — e.g. "AUTH_ERROR"
    // error.message — human-readable message
  } else if (error instanceof FluxisNetworkError) {
    // Could not connect to Fluxis API (DNS, timeout, etc.)
    // error.cause — original fetch error
  } else if (error instanceof FluxisResponseParseError) {
    // API returned a non-JSON response (e.g. HTML error page from a proxy)
    // error.rawBody — the raw response body string
    // error.statusCode — HTTP status code
    // error.method, error.path — request context
  } else if (error instanceof FluxisError) {
    // API returned a structured error response
    // error.code — e.g. "AK0001"
    // error.message — includes "METHOD /path: message" prefix
    // error.details — additional context
    // error.statusCode — HTTP status code
    // error.method, error.path — request context
  }
}
```

Error class hierarchy:
- `FluxisError` (base) — all API errors. Fields: `code`, `message`, `details`, `statusCode`, `method`, `path`.
  - `FluxisAuthError` — authentication failures (401)
  - `FluxisNetworkError` — connection/timeout issues
  - `FluxisResponseParseError` — non-JSON responses (proxy errors, HTML pages). Additional field: `rawBody`.

---

## Complete Example: Express Server with Payments and Webhooks

```typescript
import express from 'express';
import { FluxisClient, FluxisError, verifyWebhookSignature } from '@fluxisus/sdk';

const app = express();

const fluxis = new FluxisClient({
  apiKey: process.env.FLUXIS_API_KEY!,
  apiSecret: process.env.FLUXIS_API_SECRET!,
  environment: 'staging',
});

const WEBHOOK_SECRET = process.env.FLUXIS_WEBHOOK_SECRET!;
const POS_ID = process.env.FLUXIS_POS_ID!;

// Create a payment
app.post('/api/payments', express.json(), async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { amount, orderId } = req.body;

    const payment = await fluxis.pointOfSale.createPaymentRequest(POS_ID, {
      amount: String(amount),
      uniqueAssetId: 'npolygon_t0x3c499c542cEF5E3811e1192ce70d8cC03d5c3359',
      referenceId: orderId,
    });

    res.json({
      paymentId: payment.id,
      token: payment.token,       // Render as QR code on frontend
      expiresAt: payment.expiration,
    });
  } catch (error) {
    if (error instanceof FluxisError) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: error.message, code: error.code });
    } else {
      res.status(500).json({ error: 'Internal server error' });
    }
  }
});

// Receive webhook
app.post('/webhooks/fluxis', express.raw({ type: 'application/json' }), async (req, res) => {
  const signature = req.headers['x-fluxis-signature'] as string;
  const rawBody = req.body.toString();

  const isValid = await verifyWebhookSignature(rawBody, signature, WEBHOOK_SECRET);
  if (!isValid) {
    return res.status(401).send('Invalid signature');
  }

  const event = JSON.parse(rawBody);

  if (event.status === 'completed' || event.status === 'overpaid') {
    // Mark order as paid in your database
    console.log(`Payment ${event.id} completed for reference ${event.reference_id}`);
  }

  res.status(200).send('OK');
});

// Check payment status (polling fallback)
app.get('/api/payments/:paymentId/status', async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const status = await fluxis.pointOfSale.getPaymentRequest(POS_ID, req.params.paymentId);
    res.json({ status: status.status });
  } catch (error) {
    if (error instanceof FluxisError) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: error.message });
    } else {
      res.status(500).json({ error: 'Internal server error' });
    }
  }
});

app.listen(3000, () => console.log('Server running on port 3000'));
```

---

## Going from Staging to Production

1. Get production API keys from the Fluxis dashboard (prefix: `fxs.prd.*`)
2. Change `environment` from `'staging'` to `'production'`
3. Update webhook URLs to your production server
4. Re-create notification settings on production PoS (webhook secrets are per-environment)

```typescript
const fluxis = new FluxisClient({
  apiKey: 'fxs.prd.xxx',
  apiSecret: 'your-production-secret',
  environment: 'production', // Points to https://api.fluxis.us/v1
});
```

---

## Configuration Reference

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|
| `apiKey` | `string` | required | Fluxis API key |
| `apiSecret` | `string` | required | Fluxis API secret |
| `environment` | `'staging' \| 'production'` | `'staging'` | API environment |
| `baseUrl` | `string` | — | Custom base URL (overrides environment) |
| `timeout` | `number` | `30000` | Request timeout in milliseconds |

---

## Key Implementation Details

- **Server-only — never use in the browser**: `FluxisClient` contains API credentials and must only run server-side (Node.js, Next.js Server Components/Route Handlers/Server Actions). In Next.js, use `import 'server-only'` (from the `server-only` npm package) in the file where you create the client to get a build-time error if it's ever imported from a Client Component.
- **camelCase ↔ snake_case**: The Fluxis API uses `snake_case` for all JSON keys. The SDK automatically converts between camelCase (TypeScript) and snake_case (API) in both directions, so you always work with idiomatic TypeScript property names.
- **Lazy auth with dedup**: Authentication happens on the first API call. Concurrent requests during token refresh share a single auth call.
- **401 auto-retry**: If a request gets a 401, the SDK invalidates the token, re-authenticates, and retries once (prevents infinite loops).
- **Safe response parsing**: 204 No Content and empty bodies are handled gracefully. Non-JSON responses (e.g. HTML from a reverse proxy) throw `FluxisResponseParseError` with the raw body preserved.
- **`AbortSignal.timeout()`**: All requests use the configured timeout (default 30s) via `AbortSignal.timeout()`.
- **`crypto.subtle`**: Webhook signature verification uses `crypto.subtle.importKey` + `HMAC-SHA256` (available in Node.js 18+ and all modern browsers).
